skill.name: typography
skill.description: Guidance for choosing distinctive fonts, strong type contrast, and effective typography pairings.
skill.summary: Helps teams improve visual quality through clear typography choices.
skill.author: Crossbill-Highlights

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported two weak cryptography alerts at SKILL.md line 3 and one system reconnaissance alert at line 5. Review found these are false positives caused by prose in typography guidance, with no executable code, commands, network access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The alert points to SKILL.md line 3, which only contains the skill description about designing typography. No cryptographic algorithm, encryption implementation, or security-sensitive code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is plain front matter description text. There is no code path or cryptographic operation to exploit.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Duplicate Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The duplicate alert also points to SKILL.md line 3 and appears to match typography wording rather than real cryptographic behavior. No evidence found of weak encryption use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The same line contains only metadata text. The repeated static hit does not add independent evidence of a security issue.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The alert points to SKILL.md line 5, which is a design statement about typography signaling quality. No system inspection command, environment lookup, or host data collection is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is natural language guidance. The file contains no shell commands, scripts, filesystem reads, or reconnaissance instructions.

content.value_statement: Generic font choices can make a polished interface feel ordinary. This skill gives clear typography rules for stronger contrast, better pairings, and more distinctive visual design.
actual_capabilities: Recommends distinctive font families for code, editorial, technical, and expressive designs.
Guides font pairing through contrast between display, serif, sans, and monospace styles.
Encourages decisive weight differences and large size jumps for visual hierarchy.
Warns against common default font choices that can make designs feel generic.
Suggests using Google Fonts as a practical source for typography implementation.
limitations: Does not include font licensing checks beyond suggesting Google Fonts.
Does not provide accessibility measurements for readability or contrast.
Does not generate complete design systems or CSS tokens by itself.
Does not test how selected fonts render across browsers or operating systems.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Product Interface
use_cases.description: Help a product designer replace generic typography with a stronger font pairing and clearer hierarchy.

use_cases.title: Shape a Technical Brand
use_cases.description: Guide a founder toward typography that feels technical, credible, and distinctive without overcomplicating the visual system.

use_cases.title: Review Website Typography
use_cases.description: Help a frontend developer assess font choices, hierarchy, and pairing decisions before shipping a page.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a Font Pair
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple typography direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend a distinctive font pairing for a modern product page. Include one display choice, one body choice, and the reason for the contrast.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Page Hierarchy
prompt_templates.scenario: Review an existing page with weak visual contrast.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page typography and suggest stronger size, weight, and font pairing choices. Focus on hierarchy and readability.

prompt_templates.title: Define a Brand Typography Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a focused typography style for a new brand.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a typography direction for a technical brand. Recommend font families, weight ranges, scale jumps, and use cases for each type style.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Design System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of typography decisions across an interface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this design system typography. Identify generic choices, weak contrast, inconsistent hierarchy, and opportunities for a more distinctive font system.

output_examples.input: A SaaS dashboard uses Inter for every heading, label, and paragraph.
output_examples.output: Use a stronger pairing. Keep a practical sans for dense UI text, then add a distinctive display face for key headings and empty states.

output_examples.input: A technical blog needs a more memorable type direction.
output_examples.output: Pair a precise monospace for code references with an editorial serif for article titles. Use large title jumps and lighter body weights.

output_examples.input: A landing page feels flat even after color and spacing changes.
output_examples.output: Increase type contrast first. Move from small weight changes to clear differences, such as light body text against bold display headings.

best_practices: Choose one distinctive font and use it consistently across important brand moments.
Create clear contrast through font category, weight, and size instead of small style changes.
Verify font loading, fallback behavior, and readability before final implementation.
anti_patterns: Using only default system fonts when the design needs a stronger brand signal.
Mixing many expressive fonts without a clear hierarchy or role for each one.
Relying on minor weight changes that users cannot easily notice.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps choose better typography through font selection, contrast, pairing, and hierarchy guidance.

faq.question: Does it create CSS automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides typography direction that a designer or developer can convert into implementation details.

faq.question: Can it recommend Google Fonts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly suggests loading distinctive choices from Google Fonts.

faq.question: Is this only for marketing pages?
faq.answer: No. It can support product interfaces, technical pages, editorial content, and brand systems.

faq.question: Does it check accessibility?
faq.answer: No. Accessibility checks should be performed separately for readability, contrast, and responsive behavior.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
